notes: Allows passwords to include special characters while keeping 8-16 character length, at least one letter, at least one number, and no spaces.
Adds a Windows-installer-style Update Now progress popup with stage indicators and a progress bar.
Automatically refreshes the browser after update completion and then shows an Update Complete popup with the new version.
Moves Reliable Hubs branding into a dedicated navbar brand block so the official logo remains visible on crowded screens.
Redesigns the Update Center layout to use wide screens better and improve readability.
Displays dates and times as separate local date, local time, and timezone values instead of compact ISO strings.
Adds automatic progress monitoring after Update Now, including clearer guidance on when it is safe to refresh the browser.
Updates the Windows Update Agent package to report download, restart, validation, completion, and rollback progress back to OrbitSM.
Replaces the temporary Reliable Hubs placeholder mark with the official Reliable Hubs logo in the OrbitSM navbar.
Removes the attachment count cap and replaces it with an add-more attachment picker while preserving the 5 MB per attachment limit.
Adds safe user deactivation/reactivation so departed employees cannot log in while historical records remain intact.
Adds project archive/restore behavior so wrongly created projects can be hidden without deleting incidents, tasks, problems, changes, or audit history.
Records project archival in related work-item audit trails and excludes archived projects from normal user pickers.
Adds weekly AI training snapshots that summarize incidents, tasks, problems, changes, and readable attachment text for better operational insights.
Adds manual AI training from Admin and surfaces source counts, top terms, and attachment evidence counts.
Enhances AI clustering and situation analysis by using readable incident attachment evidence from uploaded logs, CSV, JSON, text, Markdown, and similar files.
Adds Reliable Hubs co-branding to the OrbitSM navigation bar.
Adds a Docker Hub publish script for versioning the full OrbitSM install package ZIP as bosesanjit/orbitsm-install-package:<version>.
Adds assignee autocomplete with name and email matching from login users and project team members.
Sends and logs notification attempts when incidents and tasks are assigned, updated, or commented.
Adds full task detail pages with comments, updates, and read-only audit history.
Adds incident comments and read-only audit history to incident detail pages.
Adds NEED_MORE_INFO incident status: SLA pauses and the incident automatically reassigns to the submitter.
Adds standardized approver, collaborator, and watcher fields across incidents, tasks, service requests, problems, and changes.
Shows operational approver/collaborators/watchers on detail pages while preserving change L1/CAB approval history separately.
Sends work-item notification attempts to valid email recipients listed as approver, collaborators, or watchers.
Adds startup schema locking to prevent fresh-database table creation races with multiple backend workers.
Update Now now refreshes the manifest and queues immediate execution; repaired Windows agent scripts poll every 1 minute.
Fixes the 1.0.12 backend startup failure caused by Python 3.11 f-string parsing in notification logging.
Adds Assigned to autocomplete on new incident creation.
Automatically fills incident creator from the logged-in account email.
Adds autocomplete-assisted selection for approvers, collaborators, and watchers.

Expands work-item notification routing so submitters/requesters, assignees, owners, approvers, collaborators, watchers, and resolved managers receive email updates when their names or emails are connected to incidents, tasks, service requests, problems, or changes.
Adds multiple attachments across incidents, tasks, problems, and changes with a maximum of 5 attachments per record and 5 MB per file.
Persists uploaded attachments in a Docker volume so files survive image updates and container recreation.
Adds attachment upload and delete audit entries plus work-item notification triggers.
Displays login retry count and locks accounts for 20 minutes after 5 failed attempts.
Extends password reset link validity from 1 hour to 24 hours.
Confirms pre-update PostgreSQL backup is required by default before patch installation.
Preserves database volumes during update and rollback; rollback changes application images only unless an explicit database restore is requested.
Adds owning project assignment and reassignment for Problems and Changes.
Records Problem and Change project transfers in read-only audit history, including old project code and new project code.
Problem and Change project filtering now includes directly assigned owning projects as well as linked incident/problem relationships.
